journal/activity_timeline.name: Activity Timeline
journal/activity_timeline.description: Infer recent activity from journal entries and optionally deeper signals. Returns a structured timeline with events, gaps, and relative timestamps. Use for understanding what happened during a time window.

target_date.description: Journal date YYYY-MM-DD (default: inferred from since)

context/chrome_activity.name: Chrome Activity
context/chrome_activity.description: Query Chrome browsing history from the rolling tab ledger. Supports: hot_tabs (ranked by engagement), changes (opened/closed/navigated/engaged/moved), sessions (domain clusters), tabs_at (snapshot at a time), context (tab proximity and window layout), details (full URLs by filter), status (ledger health). Output is compact (no URLs) — use details query for full URLs.

query.description: Query type: hot_tabs, changes, sessions, tabs_at, context, details, status (default: hot_tabs)

since.description: Start of window. Relative ('2h', '1d') or ISO datetime (default: 2h)

timestamp.description: For tabs_at/details queries: ISO datetime or relative shorthand

filter.description: For details query: domain or title substring to match

context/chrome_cluster.name: Chrome Cluster
context/chrome_cluster.description: Cluster currently-open Chrome tabs by semantic similarity. Extracts page content, embeds with document-tower model, and clusters via Louvain. Completely free — no LLM calls. Returns tab groups with cohesion scores. Set use_content=false for title-only clustering (faster, works when extension can't extract).

use_content.description: True: extract+embed page text (richer). False: embed titles only (faster). Default: true.

max_extract.description: Max tabs to extract content from (default 30)

max_chars.description: Max chars per tab for extraction (default 3000)

context/chrome_content.name: Chrome Content
context/chrome_content.description: Extract full page text from currently-open Chrome tabs. Filter by domain or title substring, or get top-engagement tabs. Free — no LLM calls. Use for single-tab inspection or reading specific page content.

tab_filter.description: Domain or title substring to match (e.g., 'github', 'obsidian'). If not set, returns top-engagement tabs.

context/chrome_infer.name: Chrome Infer
context/chrome_infer.description: Infer what the user is working on by reading page content from engaged Chrome tabs and analyzing with Haiku. Evaluates provided theories against actual page evidence. Caches results per tab to avoid redundant API calls. ~$0.001/call.

since.description: Lookback window. Relative ('1h', '30m') or ISO datetime. Default: 1h.

theories.description: Comma-separated theories to evaluate (e.g., 'researching pricing, writing code')

context/chrome_tab_close.name: Chrome Tab Close
context/chrome_tab_close.description: Close specified Chrome tabs by tab ID. Returns count of closed/missing tabs. Use after triage decisions.

tab_ids.description: List of Chrome tab IDs (integers) to close

context/chrome_tab_group.name: Chrome Tab Group
context/chrome_tab_group.description: Create a Chrome tab group or add tabs to an existing group. Returns the group ID.

tab_ids.description: List of Chrome tab IDs to group

color.description: Group color: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan, orange

group_id.description: Existing group ID to add to (omit to create new group)

context/chrome_tab_move.name: Chrome Tab Move
context/chrome_tab_move.description: Move Chrome tabs to a specific position or window.

tab_ids.description: List of Chrome tab IDs to move

window_id.description: Target window ID (omit for current window)

notifications/consent/consent_grant.name: Consent Grant
notifications/consent/consent_grant.description: LOW-LEVEL: Direct consent grant for deferred resolution ONLY. Do NOT use this to bypass the consent flow — use consent_request instead, which notifies the user and waits for their approval. This capability exists for: (1) manual resolution after a consent_request timeout when the user later approves out-of-band, (2) programmatic grants from surface callbacks. All grants are session-scoped. Modes: 'always' (24h), 'temporary' (TTL-based), 'once' (single-use).

operation.description: Operation identifier from the consent_required response

notifications/consent/consent_list.name: Consent List
notifications/consent/consent_list.description: List all consent entries with their status (mode, tier, expiry for temporary grants).

notifications/consent/consent_request.name: Consent Request
notifications/consent/consent_request.description: One-call consent flow: create a consent request, deliver to all available surfaces, and optionally poll + auto-resolve. The modal shows Allow always / Allow for N min / Allow once / Deny options. Without timeout_seconds: non-blocking (returns request_id for later polling via request_poll + consent_request_resolve). With timeout_seconds: blocks until user responds, then auto-resolves (writes the grant on approval, returns denial on deny). Max recommended timeout: 110s to stay within MCP limits.

default_ttl.description: Default TTL in minutes for temporary grants

context.description: Optional metadata shown in the modal

timeout_seconds.description: Poll timeout. Omit for non-blocking. Max recommended: 110s

surfaces.description: Target surface names (e.g., ['dashboard']). Default: all available

notifications/consent/consent_request_re.name: Consent Request Resolve
notifications/consent/consent_request_re.description: Approve or deny a pending consent request. If approved, writes the grant and dispatches the callback (session resume or messaging).

approved.description: True to approve, False to deny

notifications/consent/consent_revoke.name: Consent Revoke
notifications/consent/consent_revoke.description: Revoke a previously granted consent for an operation.

context/context_bundle.name: Context Bundle
context/context_bundle.description: Run all (or selected) collectors and save a context bundle to disk. Use individual collectors (context_git, context_chat, etc.) when you only need one source.

days.description: Override all time windows to N days

hours.description: Override all time windows to N hours

context/context_calendar.name: Context Calendar
context/context_calendar.description: Google Calendar schedule for a given date. Also checks plugin readiness.

check_ready.description: Return only readiness check, no schedule fetch (default false)

context/context_chat.name: Context Chat
context/context_chat.description: Recent Claude Code conversations and CLI history with tool usage, duration, and outcome snippets

context/context_chrome.name: Context Chrome
context/context_chrome.description: Currently open Chrome tabs (requires Chrome extension running)

context/context_git.name: Context Git
context/context_git.description: Recent git activity across all repos: commits, diffs, dirty trees. Pass annotate=true to tag commits made by agent sessions with their session ID.

dirty_only.description: Only repos with uncommitted changes (default false)

annotate.description: Tag commits made by agent sessions with session ID (default false). Slower — scans JSONL files.

context/context_messages.name: Context Messages
context/context_messages.description: Inter-agent messaging state: pending, recent, unread messages

context/context_obsidian.name: Context Obsidian
context/context_obsidian.description: Obsidian vault summary: journal entries, recently modified notes

context/context_projects.name: Context Projects
context/context_projects.description: Active projects with identity, state, and trajectory — synthesized from vault directories, STATE.md files in repos, task tags, git activity, and contracts

context/context_search.name: Context Search
context/context_search.description: Search indexed content (conversations, documents, tabs). Requires IR index — build with ir_index first. Methods: 'substring' (exact match, no embedding service), 'keyword' (BM25), 'semantic' (dense), or comma-delimited combo like 'keyword,semantic' (default, RRF fused).

source.description: Filter by source type (e.g. 'conversation'). Default: all sources.

scope.description: Narrow to a specific item within a source (e.g. a session_id for conversations, a tab_id for Chrome tabs). Uses doc_id prefix matching.

method.description: Search method(s). 'substring' (exact, no service needed), 'keyword' (BM25), 'semantic' (dense), or comma-delimited like 'keyword,semantic' (default). substring is solo-only.

recency.description: Apply recency bias to favor recent results (default true). Set false to rank purely by text relevance.

context/context_smart.name: Context Smart
context/context_smart.description: Smart Connections context: semantically related notes to active contracts

context/context_tasks.name: Context Tasks
context/context_tasks.description: Obsidian task summary: outstanding tasks + recent state changes (last 48h by default)

journal_days.description: Days of journal entries to scan (default 7)

event_hours.description: Hours of task state history to include (default 48, from config). Pass 0 to suppress.

context/context_wellness.name: Context Wellness
context/context_wellness.description: Wellness tracker summary from recent journal entries

contracts/contract_constraints.name: Contract Constraints
contracts/contract_constraints.description: Get active contracts with their current bottleneck constraints

contracts/contract_health.name: Contract Health
contracts/contract_health.description: Health check report: status counts, overdue, stale, missing fields

contracts/contract_wip_check.name: Contract Wip Check
contracts/contract_wip_check.description: Check if active contract count is within the WIP limit (max 3)

contracts/contracts_summary.name: Contracts Summary
contracts/contracts_summary.description: Markdown summary of all contracts with title, status, deadline, progress

context/datacore_compile_plan.name: Datacore Compile Plan
context/datacore_compile_plan.description: Compile a structured JSON query plan into a Datacore query string. Plan keys: target (required), path, tags, tags_any, status, text_contains, exists, frontmatter, child_of, parent, expressions, negate.

plan_json.description: JSON string of the query plan

context/datacore_evaluate.name: Datacore Evaluate
context/datacore_evaluate.description: Evaluate a Datacore expression (e.g. arithmetic, field access).

context/datacore_fullquery.name: Datacore Fullquery
context/datacore_fullquery.description: Execute a Datacore query with timing and revision metadata. Same as datacore_query but includes duration_s and revision.

context/datacore_get_page.name: Datacore Get Page
context/datacore_get_page.description: Get a single vault page by path with Datacore metadata: frontmatter, sections, tags, links, timestamps.

context/datacore_query.name: Datacore Query
context/datacore_query.description: Execute a Datacore query against the vault index. Supports @page, @section, @block, @task, @list-item, @codeblock with filters like path(), tags, childof(), parentof(). Returns serialized results.

fields.description: Comma-separated fields to include (e.g. '$path,$tags'). Default: all.

context/datacore_run_plan.name: Datacore Run Plan
context/datacore_run_plan.description: Compile and execute a structured query plan in one step. Preferred over raw datacore_query when building queries programmatically — the plan schema is simpler and validates before execution.

plan_json.description: JSON string of the query plan

context/datacore_schema.name: Datacore Schema
context/datacore_schema.description: Summarize the vault's Datacore schema: object types, top tags, frontmatter keys, path prefixes. Use before building queries to understand what's available.

context/datacore_status.name: Datacore Status
context/datacore_status.description: Check if Datacore plugin is installed, initialized, and queryable. Returns version, index revision, and object type counts.

context/datacore_validate.name: Datacore Validate
context/datacore_validate.description: Validate a Datacore query string without executing it. Returns parse error details if invalid.

journal/day_planner.name: Day Planner
journal/day_planner.description: Day Planner operations: check plugin status, read current plan, generate schedule from events+tasks, or write plan to journal. Composite: replaces separate check_ready/get_plan/generate/write/resync calls.

target.description: Date target for read/write. Default: today.

calendar_events.description: For generate: JSON list of calendar events

focused_tasks.description: For generate: JSON list of task dicts with 'description' key

config_overrides.description: JSON dict of day_planner config overrides

status/feature_status.name: Feature Status
status/feature_status.description: Show which tools, features, and capabilities are available or disabled, and why. Use this to diagnose missing integrations.

verbose.description: Include probe timing and config details

messaging/get_thread.name: Get Thread
messaging/get_thread.description: Get all messages in a conversation thread

journal/hot_files.name: Hot Files
journal/hot_files.description: Rank vault files by activity intensity, fusing modification frequency (vault events) with writing intensity (Keep the Rhythm). Hierarchically collapses busy directories to prevent context flooding. Use sub_directory to drill into a specific area.

sub_directory.description: Vault-relative path to drill into (e.g. 'repos/work-buddy'). Shows file-level detail.

collapse_threshold.description: Max files per directory before collapsing (default 5)

context/ir_index.name: Ir Index
context/ir_index.description: Build or check the IR search index. Run 'build' to index conversations for semantic search. Run 'status' to check index health.

days.description: Index sessions from last N days (default 30)

journal/journal_sign_in.name: Journal Sign In
journal/journal_sign_in.description: Read sign-in state (sleep/energy/mood/check-in/motto) and wellness trends, optionally write fields. Composite: replaces separate extract_sign_in + interpret_wellness + write_sign_in calls.

target.description: Date target: 'today', 'yesterday', or YYYY-MM-DD. Default: today.

write_fields.description: JSON dict of fields to write (e.g. {"sleep": 7, "mood": 8}). Consent-gated. Omit for read-only.

journal/journal_state.name: Journal State
journal/journal_state.description: Read journal state: target date, activity window, existing entries

journal/journal_write.name: Journal Write
journal/journal_write.description: Append log entries or persist a briefing to the journal. For log entries: pass time/description tuples. For briefing: pass markdown to wrap in a callout.

entries.description: For log_entries: JSON list of [time, description] tuples

status/list_sessions.name: List Sessions
status/list_sessions.description: List all known agent sessions with metadata

status/llm_call.name: Llm Call
status/llm_call.description: Make a single LLM API call (Tier 2 execution). Cheaper than spawning a full agent session. Supports freeform text or structured JSON output via output_schema (inline dict or named schema from work_buddy/llm/schemas/). Handles caching, cost tracking, and model tier selection automatically.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema for structured output. Pass a dict for inline schemas, or a string name to load from work_buddy/llm/schemas/<name>.json. Omit for freeform text.

cache_ttl_minutes.description: Cache TTL in minutes. None=config default, 0=no cache.

status/llm_costs.name: Llm Costs
status/llm_costs.description: Check LLM token usage, costs, and breakdown for this session. Shows per-task costs, per-model costs, cache hit rates, and top callers.

breakdown.description: Show per-task and per-model breakdown (default: false)

status/mcp_registry_reload.name: Mcp Registry Reload
status/mcp_registry_reload.description: Invalidate and rebuild the capability registry. Use after code changes to pick up new capabilities without restarting the MCP server.

memory/memory_prune.name: Memory Prune
memory/memory_prune.description: Delete memories from the bank. CONSENT-GATED, IRREVERSIBLE. Call with no args to list documents for review. Then provide document_id to delete a specific document's memories, or memory_type to bulk-delete a category (world/experience/observation).

document_id.description: Delete a specific document and its derived memories

memory_type.description: Bulk delete by type: world, experience, or observation

memory/memory_read.name: Memory Read
memory/memory_read.description: Read from personal memory (Hindsight). No LLM cost. Modes: 'search' (default) — semantic + keyword recall, use descriptive topic phrases with specific entity names for best results; 'model' — fetch a mental model by ID; 'recent' — list latest memories.

query.description: Descriptive topic phrase for search mode. Use specific terminology and entity names for best results (e.g. 'scope fusion and branch explosion patterns' rather than 'blindspots'). Ignored for model/recent modes.

mode.description: search (default) — semantic + keyword recall. model — fetch a mental model (self-profile, work-patterns, blindspots, preferences, current-constraints). recent — list N most recent memories.

model_id.description: Mental model ID for mode=model (default: self-profile)

budget.description: Retrieval depth for mode=search: low (fast, default), mid, high

memory/memory_reflect.name: Memory Reflect
memory/memory_reflect.description: LLM-powered reasoning over memories. CONSENT-GATED: triggers a server-side LLM call against your Anthropic API key (~1-3K tokens per call). Use memory_read for free retrieval first.

query.description: Question to reason about using memory

memory/memory_write.name: Memory Write
memory/memory_write.description: Store a personal fact, preference, or constraint in memory

content.description: The fact or preference to remember

kind.description: Memory kind: preference, habit, constraint, blindspot, relationship, decision, life-context (default preference)

notifications/notification_list_pending.name: Notification List Pending
notifications/notification_list_pending.description: List all pending notifications and requests awaiting user response.

notifications/notification_send.name: Notification Send
notifications/notification_send.description: Send a fire-and-forget notification to the user via all available surfaces (Obsidian, Telegram if enabled). No response expected. Optionally target specific surfaces.

projects/project_create.name: Project Create
projects/project_create.description: Manually create a project that the collector can't discover (books, businesses, admin workflows, etc.)

projects/project_delete.name: Project Delete
projects/project_delete.description: Delete a project from the identity registry (consent-gated). Hindsight memories are preserved.

projects/project_discover.name: Project Discover
projects/project_discover.description: Discover project candidates from task tags and git repos not yet in the registry. Returns candidates for agent review — evaluate each and use project_create to promote real projects.

projects/project_get.name: Project Get
projects/project_get.description: Get a single project with its recent observations (identity + state + trajectory)

projects/project_list.name: Project List
projects/project_list.description: List all projects with observation counts, optionally filtered by status

status.description: Filter by status: active, paused, past, future, inferred

projects/project_memory.name: Project Memory
projects/project_memory.description: Read from the project memory bank (Hindsight-backed). Modes: 'search' (semantic recall, optionally scoped to one project), 'model' (fetch a mental model: project-landscape, active-risks, recent-decisions, inter-project-deps), 'recent' (latest project memories)

model_id.description: Mental model ID for mode=model (default: project-landscape)

project.description: Project slug to scope search (omit for cross-project)

projects/project_observe.name: Project Observe
projects/project_observe.description: Record an observation about a project — strategic decisions, supervisor feedback, pivots, blockers, or anything that shapes trajectory but wouldn't appear in code or tasks

content.description: The observation — what happened, what it means, what changed

projects/project_update.name: Project Update
projects/project_update.description: Update a project's identity: name, status, or description

status.description: New status: active, paused, past, future, inferred

messaging/query_messages.name: Query Messages
messaging/query_messages.description: Query messages by recipient, sender, status, or limit

messaging/read_message.name: Read Message
messaging/read_message.description: Fetch a single message with full body content

status/remote_session_begin.name: Remote Session Begin
status/remote_session_begin.description: Launch or resume a visible Claude Code session in a real terminal window. If session_id or session_name is provided, resumes that session; otherwise starts a new one. Designed for Remote Control (phone app) connection.

cwd.description: Working directory. Defaults to repo root.

prompt.description: Initial prompt for a new session. Ignored when resuming.

session_id.description: Session ID to resume. Triggers resume mode.

session_name.description: Session name to look up for resume. Triggers resume mode.

bypass_permissions.description: Add --dangerously-skip-permissions so the session operates without interactive permission prompts. Default: True.

status/remote_session_list.name: Remote Session List
status/remote_session_list.description: List resumable Claude Code sessions from ~/.claude/sessions/. Shows session ID, name, cwd, and start time.

cwd.description: Filter to sessions started in this directory. Defaults to repo root.

msg_id.description: ID of message to reply to

notifications/request_poll.name: Request Poll
notifications/request_poll.description: Check/wait for a response to a previously delivered request. Without timeout_seconds: single immediate check. With timeout_seconds: blocks until response or timeout (max recommended: 110s). Response is cleared from Obsidian after reading (one-shot).

timeout_seconds.description: Poll timeout. Omit for immediate check. Max recommended: 110s

notifications/request_send.name: Request Send
notifications/request_send.description: Create a request, deliver to all available surfaces, and optionally poll for the user's response. Supports choice, boolean, freeform, and range response types. Without timeout_seconds: non-blocking (returns immediately, use request_poll later). With timeout_seconds: blocks until response or timeout (max recommended: 110s to stay within MCP call limits).

custom_template.description: For custom type: surface-specific rendering data (e.g., {type: 'triage_clarify', presentation: ...})

timeout_seconds.description: Poll timeout. Omit for non-blocking. Max recommended: 110s

journal/running_notes.name: Running Notes
journal/running_notes.description: Read the Running Notes section from the user's daily journal. This is the primary stream-of-consciousness capture zone where the user records ideas, observations, and notes throughout the day. Supports filtering by date range, last N days, or same-day only. Call with same_day=true for just today's entries, or days=N for recent history.

same_day.description: Only notes from the journal's own date (no carried-over content)

days.description: Most recent N days (today=1). Cannot combine with start/stop.

start.description: Include notes from this date onward (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive)

stop.description: Include notes up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive)

messaging/send_message.name: Send Message
messaging/send_message.description: Send a message to another agent or project

type.description: Message type: status-update, question, result, escalation

thread_id.description: Thread ID to continue a conversation

status/service_health.name: Service Health
status/service_health.description: Check if the messaging service is running

status/session_activity.name: Session Activity
status/session_activity.description: Query the session activity ledger — what this agent session has done through work-buddy. Filters by event type, capability, category, status. Returns last N matching entries (newest first).

capability_name.description: Filter to a specific capability name

category.description: Filter by category (tasks, journal, context, etc.)

status.description: Filter by status: ok, error, consent_required

context/session_commits.name: Session Commits
context/session_commits.description: Extract git commits made during Claude Code sessions. Parses raw JSONL for Bash tool calls containing 'git commit' and their results. Scope to one session or scan all recent sessions.

session_id.description: Full or partial session UUID. If omitted, scans all recent sessions.

days.description: Lookback window when scanning all sessions (default 7)

context/session_expand.name: Session Expand
context/session_expand.description: Full context around a specific message in a session. Returns untruncated text for the target and surrounding messages.

context/session_get.name: Session Get
context/session_get.description: Browse messages in a Claude Code session. Paginated with role/type filtering. Use after context_search finds a session.

context/session_locate.name: Session Locate
context/session_locate.description: Jump from a context_search hit to the relevant conversation page. Takes a span_index from search result metadata and returns messages centered on that chunk.

span_index.description: IR span index from context_search result metadata

context/session_search.name: Session Search
context/session_search.description: Hybrid search within a single session. Uses IR (keyword/semantic/substring) scoped to the session, then resolves chunk hits to message-level results via the span map.

status/session_summary.name: Session Summary
status/session_summary.description: Compact summary of what this agent session has done — counts by category/capability, errors, mutations, key artifacts created, workflow progress.

context/session_uncommitted.name: Session Uncommitted
context/session_uncommitted.description: Find agent sessions that wrote files still present in dirty git state. Answers: 'which sessions wrote code that was never committed?' Cross-references Write/Edit/NotebookEdit tool calls against git status --porcelain across all repos.
aliases: what did the agent write but not commit

context/session_wb_activity.name: Session Wb Activity
context/session_wb_activity.description: Summary of what a session did through work-buddy's MCP gateway — capabilities invoked, workflows run, errors, key artifacts. Reads from the per-session activity ledger.

session_id.description: Session ID to query. Default: current session.

status/setup_help.name: Setup Help
status/setup_help.description: Diagnose why a component isn't working. Runs automated check sequences that walk dependency chains and stop at the first failure with a root cause and fix suggestion. Use 'all' for an overview of all components, or specify a component ID (e.g. 'hindsight', 'obsidian', 'postgresql') for targeted diagnostics.

component.description: Component ID to diagnose, or 'all' for overview. Available: postgresql, obsidian, hindsight, chrome_extension, messaging, embedding, telegram, dashboard, smart_connections, datacore, google_calendar

status/sidecar_jobs.name: Sidecar Jobs
status/sidecar_jobs.description: List all scheduled sidecar jobs with their next fire time, heartbeat status, and whether exclusion windows are active.

status/sidecar_status.name: Sidecar Status
status/sidecar_status.description: Check if the sidecar daemon is running and get its current state: supervised services health, scheduler status, and upcoming job schedule.

contracts/stale_contracts.name: Stale Contracts
contracts/stale_contracts.description: List contracts not reviewed in N days (default 7)

status/tailscale_status.name: Tailscale Status
status/tailscale_status.description: Check Tailscale VPN status: daemon state, tailnet identity, online peers, and Serve configuration (published ports).

tasks/task_archive.name: Task Archive
tasks/task_archive.description: Move completed tasks from master list to tasks/archive.md

older_than_days.description: Only archive tasks done N+ days ago (default 0 = all)

tasks/task_assign.name: Task Assign
tasks/task_assign.description: Claim a task for the current session and get full context (text, note, metadata)

tasks/task_briefing.name: Task Briefing
tasks/task_briefing.description: Daily task status summary with contract constraints, MITs, focused, overdue, stale, suggestions

tasks/task_change_state.name: Task Change State
tasks/task_change_state.description: Update task metadata: state, urgency, due date (any combo)

state.description: New state: inbox, mit, focused, snoozed, done

tasks/task_create.name: Task Create
tasks/task_create.description: Create a new task in the master task list. Optionally attach a note file for details/subtasks.

task_text.description: Short single-line task description (NO newlines — will be rejected)

summary.description: If provided, creates a linked note file with this summary

tasks/task_delete.name: Task Delete
tasks/task_delete.description: Permanently delete a task: remove line, note file, and store record. Consent-gated.

tasks/task_review_inbox.name: Task Review Inbox
tasks/task_review_inbox.description: Get inbox tasks with suggested actions (mit, snooze, kill, needs_date)

tasks/task_scattered.name: Task Scattered
tasks/task_scattered.description: Find open tasks scattered across the vault outside the master task list. Groups by file with counts. Uses Datacore structural queries.

tasks/task_stale_check.name: Task Stale Check
tasks/task_stale_check.description: Find forgotten/stale tasks across inbox, snoozed, MIT, and focused

tasks/task_sync.name: Task Sync
tasks/task_sync.description: Compare master task list against SQLite store: detect orphans, create missing store records, report checkbox mismatches

tasks/task_toggle.name: Task Toggle
tasks/task_toggle.description: Toggle a task between TODO and DONE (checkbox, done date, store state). Consent-gated.

threads/thread_ask.name: Thread Ask
threads/thread_ask.description: Ask a question in a thread and optionally wait for the user's response.

threads/thread_create.name: Thread Create
threads/thread_create.description: Create a new conversation thread with the user. Opens a chat sidebar on the dashboard.

threads/thread_poll.name: Thread Poll
threads/thread_poll.description: Check if the latest question in a thread has been answered.

threads/thread_send.name: Thread Send
threads/thread_send.description: Send a message in an existing thread (fire-and-forget, no response expected).

context/triage_execute.name: Triage Execute
context/triage_execute.description: Execute triage decisions from the review view. Takes the user's Phase 2 response (group_decisions, reassignments) and the original presentation, then performs all actions: close tabs, create tasks, record into tasks, organize tab groups.

presentation.description: The original presentation dict (for item metadata)

context/triage_item_detail.name: Triage Item Detail
context/triage_item_detail.description: Retrieve the Haiku summary and/or raw content for a specific triage item. Use during triage review to inspect items with content gaps. Works for any source (Chrome tabs, journal entries, conversations). Requires a prior triage pipeline run.

include_raw.description: Also return raw content (page text, etc.). Default: false — prefer summaries.

messaging/update_message_status.name: Update Message Status
messaging/update_message_status.description: Update a message's status (e.g., pending → resolved)

journal/vault_write_at_location.name: Vault Write At Location
journal/vault_write_at_location.description: Insert content at a specific section in a vault note. Configurable note (path or resolver like 'latest_journal', 'today'), section (header text), and position ('top' or 'bottom' of section). Used by Telegram capture and general-purpose vault writing.

note.description: Note path or resolver: 'latest_journal' (default), 'today', or explicit vault-relative path

section.description: Header text identifying target section (default: 'Running Notes')

tasks/weekly_review_data.name: Weekly Review Data
tasks/weekly_review_data.description: Gather all data for the weekly review: contracts, constraints, WIP, tasks, staleness, suggestions
